commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat futures near 2-year high at $6.36/bushel (+3.2% session on March 21). Up 13.2% from pre-war. Driven by energy and fertilizer cost surge, shipping disruptions, and concerns for Egypt and Middle East wheat importers relying on Hormuz routes.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn futures at $4.72/bushel (+2.5% session). Up 9.5% from pre-war $4.31. Fertilizer (urea) price now $700+/tonne, up 47% from pre-war. Biofuel demand elevated as oil prices remain high.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: CBOT soybeans at $12.30/bushel (+2.46% session). Up 21.5% from pre-war $10.12. Soybean oil hit 69.73 cents/lb (highest since December 2022). Palm oil also surged up to 10%. One-third of globally traded fertilizer passes through Hormuz.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Energy costs for refineries surging globally. Transport costs up 40% due to diesel price spikes.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Palm oil jumped 10% — shipping through Strait of Hormuz blocked. Alternative routing adds 12+ days and $933,000 per voyage.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged from $475 to $695/tonne. Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. Worst timing: start of Northern Hemisphere planting season. Wolfe Research estimates food-at-home inflation could rise ~2 percentage points. USDA predicts 3.1% food price rise in 2026.

context: Day 22 update: Houthis threaten Bab el-Mandeb closure alongside Hormuz blockade. A double chokepoint scenario would amplify food price impacts. Wheat near 2-year high, fertilizer (urea) up 35% in one week (CRU Group). Container shipping surcharges at $3,000-4,000/container. GCC states most exposed as 95% of food imports transit Hormuz.
